headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair comparison for people deciding between Super and Folk. Folk represents a class of lighter‑weight AI assistants and automation tools. Super is built for durable, repeatable computer‑use workflows, with a reusable computer‑use cache that makes repeated execution faster and cheaper over time. This page explains the difference, cites recent reporting on agentic AI, and situates both products in the broader landscape alongside ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Siri, and Orchids.
